subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #11 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Paperpie
Virtual BookFair
We are excited to partner with
PaperPie
(formerly
Usborne Books
) to host a
Virtual Book Fair
! These beautifully made, age-appropriate books make wonderful gifts for children and families.
Through this partnership, our school earns free books that we donate to local programs in need of new reading materials. In previous years, we’ve partnered with
UTCAO Preschool
(across from Home Depot) to help them expand their classroom library—and we’re proud to continue that tradition of sharing the joy of reading with others.
The link to their
online catelog is here
.
Friends of SO Public Library
Jewelry Sale
Saturday November 15, from 10am to 5pm
Sunday November 16, from 1pm to 4pm
Baird Community Center, 5 Mead Street, South Orange
The Friends of the South Orange Public Library are a dedicated group of volunteers who fundraise to enhance library programs and services for our community. They are hosting a jewelry sale at the Baird Center, and it’s a wonderful opportunity to support the library while getting a head start on your holiday shopping.
Gardening with Ms. Linda
Over the past two weeks we have planted over 125 bulbs. Except for the tulips, all the bulbs have been selected because they are deer deterrent. The snowdrops (Galanthus) will bloom first, as early as February, followed by tulips, daffodils, winter windflower (Anemone blanda) and summer 
snowflakes (Leucojum aestivum) by mid-spring. Rising 32 inches in height and adored by bees, butterflies and other pollinators, Allium purple sensation is the last of our bulbs to bloom in late spring or early summer. It has been challenging to dig and plant bulbs as the temperatures drop but we stay warm by keeping busy. We’ve been raking leaves and adding them to the compost bin to achieve a balance between “brown” and “green” materials.  It was exciting to chop up a pumpkin and examine the fungi that was growing on the inside before feeding it to the “decomposers” in the bin.
PEP Thanksgiving Placements
OLS Thanksgiving Food Distribution
Thank you to our wonderful SOCDS PEP team for organizing a meaningful opportunity for our students to spread caring throughout our community. Mary Payne (Ruby’s mom) created the beautiful placement design above, and Rachel Pattison (Luca’s mom) has coordinated the distribution to the OLS Thanksgiving Food Drive. Next week, the children will color their placements in class, and we will laminate them before they are shared.
Doing for others is a powerful way for children to understand empathy, generosity, and the impact of small acts of kindness.
Children will not sign their placements, and there will be no identification of where they came from in 
order to protect our students.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny used a variety of techniques to create dream catchers and totem poles.
An example was using a spray bottle to wet the paper and then when using crayon the effect is a tie-dye look.

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
